metadata.title:
YOU COULD KICK THIS


RAILROAD OFF THE MAP

Washington Central Branch Another Jim Hill


Death-trap.

pages.raw_text:
admitted by railroad employees and
patrons alike


When the train passed over the
rails on the rotten ties then rails
were crushed down into the ties.


The Washington Central runs to
Adrian, over 100 miles from SpO-
kane The line is a menace to life
and a disgrace to this part of the
state, both In trackage and car
equipment. Two passenger and
several freight trains pass over 1t
daily. Every trip over IL means
that passengers and trainmen
must take their lives in their hands.


The cars are out of date and un-
comfortable. but this can be en-.
dured better than the constant ex-
pectation of being crushed In a
smash-up which besets travellers
who know the road and have to
use It,


If the state railroad commission
Is in earnest about regulating traf-
fic affairs in this state so as TO pro-
vide for the safety of the travelling
public, it can find no better point of
beginning than the Washington
Central. II is death trap DELHI
erately maintained under the Hill
policy of spending nothing on road
bed as long as a train can get
through somehow.


The Diamond lake wreck is q
vivid example of what this policy
means to railroad employees and the
public
